title: dalbergia tonkinensis
intro: Dalbergia tonkinensis, also known as Tonkin rosewood or Dalbergia tonkinensis, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Dalbergia tonkinensis include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant has been used in traditional folk medicine to treat conditions such as rheumatism and arthritis, and is also valued for its timber and wood products.
